title: Versioned Migrations, Expand-Contract Changes, and Rollback Evidence · Goroutine Forensics
summary: A preserved Go feed-service run contains one plausible discovery, URL, XML, normalization, fetch, SQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, handler, accessibility, migration, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in rehearsed schema and data migration chain and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Keep ordered immutable migration files, checksums, ownership, lock behavior, and applied-state inspection under revision control.
Decide transaction boundaries from each PostgreSQL operation and separate concurrent index, long data, and lock-sensitive work.
Stage additive schema, backfill, dual-read or dual-write, validation, cutover, cleanup, and old-version compatibility.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Feed Go Migration Ledger
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 5056 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove immutable migrations, expand-contract compatibility, bounded backfill, and recovery; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep ordered immutable migration files, checksums, ownership, lock behavior, and applied-state inspection under revision control.

Inspect the Feed Go Migration Ledger trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Migration Recovery
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: feed-go-migration-recovery-4z0gyk" that uses case 6494 to prove immutable migrations, expand-contract compatibility, bounded backfill, and recovery. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, parse through native XML libraries, contact publishers, access PostgreSQL, read host files, handle real signals, launch unbounded goroutines, or claim race, load, accessibility, container, backup, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, pgx 5.10.0, goose 3.27.2, PostgreSQL 18.4, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, disposable databases, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected images, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, and release gates.
